2604.26024.title: Correcting Performance Estimation Bias in Imbalanced Classification with Minority Subconcepts
2604.26024.paper_summary: Class-level evaluation can conceal substantial performance disparities across subconcepts within the same class, causing models that perform well on average to fail on specific subpopulations. Prior work has shown that common evaluation measures for imbalanced classification are biased toward larger minority subconcepts and that utility-based reweighting using true subconcept labels can mitigate this bias; however, such labels are rarely available at test time. We introduce a practical utility-weighted evaluation that replaces unavailable subconcept labels with predicted posterior probabilities from a multiclass subconcept model. Evaluation weights are defined as the expected utility under this posterior, yielding a soft, uncertainty-aware metric we call predicted-weighted balanced accuracy (pBA). Experiments on tabular benchmarks as well as medical-imaging and text datasets show that unweighted scores can be misleading under within-class heterogeneity, while pBA provides more stable and interpretable assessments when subconcept distributions are uneven but not pathological. Our code is available at: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/correcting-bias-imbalance-9C6C/.
2604.26024.paper_summary_zh: 班級層級的評估可能隱藏同一類別內部子概念之間的顯著性能差異，導致在平均表現良好的模型在特定子群體上失敗。先前的研究顯示，對於不平衡分類的常見評估措施偏向於較大的少數子概念，而使用真實子概念標籤的效用基礎重加權可以減輕這種偏見；然而，在測試時這些標籤通常很少可用。我們提出了一種實用的效用加權評估，將不可用的子概念標籤替換為來自多類別子概念模型的預測後驗概率。評估權重被定義為在此後驗下的期望效用，從而產生一種我們稱之為預測加權平衡準確率（pBA）的軟性、不確定性意識的指標。在表格基準、醫學影像和文本數據集上的實驗顯示，在類內異質性下，未加權的分數可能會產生誤導，而pBA在子概念分佈不均但不病態的情況下提供了更穩定且可解釋的評估。我們的代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://anonymous.4open.science/r/correcting-bias-imbalance-9C6C/。
2604.26024.author: Taylor Maxson et.al.
2604.26024.authors: Taylor Maxson, Roberto Corizzo, Yaning Wu, Nathalie Japkowicz, Colin Bellinger
